conventional_view: This likely constitutes a serial meeting under Government Code § 54952.2(b)(1) because the AI system served as an intermediary through which members' positions were communicated and progressively developed into collective alignment (evidenced by the "points of alignment" brief), even though no member intended to communicate with another; the § 54952.2(b)(2) safe harbor does not apply because CivicAssist disclosed members' positions to each other rather than maintaining neutrality; and the city must likely respond to the cure-and-correct demand and may need to restart the hearing or disqualify members.
disfavored_position: The communications did not constitute a serial meeting because no member intended to communicate with or develop collective agreement with another, the AI system was not an "intermediary" within the statutory meaning, and the fact-finding function resembled permissible staff research.
